name: Idea Intake
description: Capture and catalog ideas systematically into the vault

steps.name: Quick Capture
steps.prompt: Let's capture your idea! Tell me: What problem have you noticed, or what solution are you thinking about? Also, where did this idea come from?
instructions: Get the raw idea in one sentence (problem or solution framing)
Capture the source (where the idea came from)
Don't wait - ideas decay quickly
Accept rough/incomplete ideas - they'll be refined later

inputs.label: Raw Idea
inputs.placeholder: Describe your idea in one sentence...

validation.message: Please provide a brief description of your idea

inputs.label: Source
inputs.placeholder: Where did this idea come from?
inputs.helpText: Helps track pattern of where good ideas originate

inputs.label: Source Details
inputs.placeholder: Any specific details about the source?
inputs.helpText: Optional: link, person's name, subreddit, etc.

steps.name: Problem Statement
steps.prompt: Now let's clarify the problem. Who has this problem? What pain does it cause them? How do they currently try to solve it?
instructions: Focus on the PROBLEM, not the solution
Identify a specific person/role who has this problem
Quantify the pain if possible (time wasted, money lost)
Understand current workarounds

inputs.label: Who has this problem?
inputs.placeholder: e.g., Freelance developers, small business owners...
inputs.helpText: Be specific about the target persona

inputs.label: What pain does it cause?
inputs.placeholder: Describe the frustration, time waste, or money loss...

inputs.label: How do they currently solve it?
inputs.helpText: Understanding alternatives helps position your solution

steps.name: Proposed Solution
steps.prompt: What would you build to solve this problem? Keep it to one paragraph - we'll refine this later.
instructions: Keep it high-level
One paragraph maximum
Focus on the core value, not features
Don't over-engineer at this stage

inputs.label: Proposed Solution
inputs.placeholder: A tool that... An app that... A service that...
inputs.helpText: One paragraph describing what you would build

inputs.label: Working Name
inputs.placeholder: Give your idea a short name...
inputs.helpText: This will be used for file naming (can change later)

validation.message: Use only letters, numbers, spaces, and hyphens

steps.name: Initial Signals
steps.prompt: Quick gut-check time! Have you searched for existing solutions? Would you pay for this? Do you know anyone with this exact pain?
instructions: These are quick signal checks, not deep research
Honest answers help filter ideas early
Personal pain is a strong signal
Knowing real people with the problem is validation

inputs.label: Have you searched for existing solutions?
inputs.helpText: Did you Google or check Product Hunt for alternatives?

inputs.label: Do you know someone with this exact pain?
inputs.helpText: A real person, not a hypothetical user

inputs.label: Who? (Optional)
inputs.placeholder: Name, role, or how you know them...

steps.name: Initial Triage
steps.prompt: Let me do a quick triage of this idea. I'll check three things: Is it a painful problem? Is it technically feasible with our stack? Could it generate revenue within 30 days of launch?
instructions: This is a gut-check, not deep validation
All three should be 'Yes' for fast-track
If any is 'No', idea goes to backlog for later review
Don't kill ideas here - just prioritize

inputs.label: Does this solve a painful problem?
inputs.helpText: Gut check - is this a 'hair on fire' problem?

inputs.label: Is this technically feasible with our stack?
inputs.helpText: Can we build this with Next.js, Tauri, our locked stack?

inputs.label: Could this generate revenue within 30 days of launch?
inputs.helpText: Is there a clear path to first paying customer?

steps.name: Generate Idea File
steps.prompt: I'll now create your idea file in the vault. This captures everything we've discussed in a structured format.
instructions: Generate slug from idea name (kebab-case)
Create file in _vault/backlog/ or _vault/active/ based on triage
Fill in all template sections
Add entry to _vault/IDEAS.md index

steps.name: Raw Notes
steps.prompt: Anything else you want to capture? Links, screenshots, quotes, random thoughts? I'll add them to your idea file.
instructions: This is optional but valuable
Capture any additional context
Links to discussions, articles, competitor sites
Raw quotes from people experiencing the problem

inputs.label: Raw Notes
inputs.placeholder: Dump any additional context, links, screenshots, quotes here...
inputs.helpText: Optional - any extra context you want to capture

qualityGates.description: Can explain the problem in one sentence without jargon
qualityGates.errorMessage: Problem statement must be clear and documented

qualityGates.description: Idea file exists in vault with template filled
qualityGates.errorMessage: Idea file must be created in _vault/
Idea must be indexed in IDEAS.md

qualityGates.description: Know where this idea came from

transition.message: Idea captured! Ready to validate whether this problem is worth solving.

pitfalls.fix: Always write problem statement before solution
Vault is cheap storage—capture everything, filter later
Keep intake light; validation SOP does deep analysis
Capture source immediately, even if it's 'random thought'
Search IDEAS.md before creating new file
